title: Job Openings at Karis Concept
content:
Karis Concept is a full-time Hospitality Recruitment company. We rececruit and train staff to offer professional services in the hospitality industry.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Accountant

Job Location: Abia
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage daily financial transactions, including processing invoices, posting payments, and tracking revenue.
	Prepare monthly management accounts and monthly/annual budgets in collaboration with department heads.
	Conduct monthly reconciliations of balance sheets and bank statements to ensure accuracy.
	Reporting
	Prepare accurate and timely monthly financial statements, including income statements and cash flow reports.
	Ensure compliance with local, state, and federal accounting regulations etc.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in hotel accounting or a similar role within the hospitality industry.
	ICAN is an added advantage.
	Proficiency in accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Sage) and Microsoft Excel.
	Strong understanding of accounting principles and practices specific to the hospitality sector.


Salary
N200,000 – N300,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date: 10th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: karisconcept@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Security Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Protect employer’s property and enforce laws.
	Monitor closed-circuit TV cameras and monitor alarms.
	Respond to any emergencies.
	Control visitor and employee building access.
	Conduct security checks for specified areas.
	Complete reports on anything they observed while on duty.


Application Closing Date: 5th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: karisconcept@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

3.) Sous Chef

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)

Job Duties


	Developing a cohesive menu that aligns with the restaurant’s mission, such as sustainably sourced ingredients, and the operating budget
	Training the kitchen staff team
	Educating servers about the menu and handling special requests from customers, such as substitutes for gluten or dairy allergies
	Maintaining an accurate inventory of ingredients and placing regular orders
	Monitoring the condition of equipment and ordering maintenance and repairs
	Inspecting food for quality standards before it leaves the kitchenSourcing, negotiating with and managing vendors, including food distributors
	Ensuring the kitchen meets food safety standards and complies with local health codes


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess an HND with 2 years of experience.


Application Closing Date: 10th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: karisconcept@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Sunrise is a subsidiary of Bond Group of Companies situated in Ibadan, Oyo State with business interest in supermarket, pharmacy, eatery and arcade.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR &amp; Admin Officer
Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary of Responsibilities


	Manage end-to-end recruitment and onboarding processes.
	Co-ordinate staff training and developmental programs.
	Enforce employee discipline and uphold a culture of accountability and ethical conduct amongst the staff.
	Monitor employee attendance, punctuality and timekeeping system.
	Process payroll accurately, and ensure remittance of statutory deductions.
	Oversee human resources and administrative statutory obligations, ensuring timely compliance with all government and regulatory requirements.
	Maintain a safe, healthy, and secure working environment in line with company and regulatory standards.
	Serve as the primary point of contact for employee relation, including inquiries, grievances, and HR-related matters.
	Oversee general office administration, including facility management, logistics and staff welfare e.t.c.


Academic Qualifications and Skills


	HND or BSc in Human Resource Management, Business Administration, Social Sciences, or a related field.
	Minimum of 4 years of experience in human resources and administration within a structured organization.
	A detail-oriented professional with a strong understanding of human resource systems and administrative processes.
	Excellent interpersonal, communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to foster a positive, inclusive, and compliant workplace environment aligned with company values.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and HR software/ERP systems.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to&nbsp;career.real01@gmail.com&nbsp;and&nbsp;hr.dept@bondpharm.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

HR & Admin Officer Job at Sunrise Universal Integrated Supermarket




        
    

        
        Sunrise is a subsidiary of Bond Group of Companies situated in Ibadan, Oyo State with business interest in supermarket, pharmacy, eatery and arcade.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR & Admin Officer
Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary of Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage end-to-end recruitment and onboarding processes.
	Co-ordinate staff training and developmental programs.
	Enforce employee discipline and uphold a culture of accountability and ethical conduct amongst the staff.
	Monitor employee attendance, punctuality and timekeeping system.
	Process payroll accurately, and ensure remittance of statutory deductions.
	Oversee human resources and administrative statutory obligations, ensuring timely compliance with all government and regulatory requirements.
	Maintain a safe, healthy, and secure working environment in line with company and regulatory standards.
	Serve as the primary point of contact for employee relation, including inquiries, grievances, and HR-related matters.
	Oversee general office administration, including facility management, logistics and staff welfare e.t.c.


Academic Qualifications and Skills


	HND or BSc in Human Resource Management, Business Administration, Social Sciences, or a related field.
	Minimum of 4 years of experience in human resources and administration within a structured organization.
	A detail-oriented professional with a strong understanding of human resource systems and administrative processes.
	Excellent interpersonal, communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to foster a positive, inclusive, and compliant workplace environment aligned with company values.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and HR software/ERP systems.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to career.real01@gmail.com and hr.dept@bondpharm.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Personal Assistant (PA)",
  "description": "Ren San &amp; Co. is a business support and full-service brand strategy firm. Our purpose is to help clients achieve their goals by developing and growing their brand, project, or event. We think of ourselves as more than just service providers, we are an extension of their team.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant (PA)

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Personal Assistant (PA) will provide comprehensive administrative, organisational, and personal support to the Principal.
	This role combines professional office assistance with personal and lifestyle management to ensure the smooth running of both work-related and personal activities.
	The PA will be responsible for managing schedules, coordinating communications, handling personal errands, and ensuring that daily priorities are effectively organised and executed.
	This position requires a highly proactive, dependable, and organised individual who can multitask, show initiative, and maintain professionalism and confidentiality at all times.


Key Responsibilities
Administrative and Executive Support:


	Manage the Principal&rsquo;s daily calendar, appointments, meetings, and travel arrangements.
	Screen and manage emails, phone calls, and correspondence on behalf of the Principal.
	Prepare reports, presentations, letters, and other official documents as required.
	Organise meetings, take minutes, and ensure proper follow-up on action points.
	Maintain an efficient filing and documentation system (both digital and physical).
	Handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion.


Personal Assistance and Errands:


	Run personal errands such as shopping, banking, pickups, and deliveries.
	Purchase household and personal items as required.
	Coordinate home maintenance, vendors, and service providers.
	Handle bill payments, renewals, and other personal administrative tasks.
	Manage appointments such as medical visits, salon bookings, or family-related schedules.
	Provide general day-to-day personal support to reduce the Principal&rsquo;s workload.


Communication and Stakeholder Engagement:


	Serve as the first point of contact for the Principal&rsquo;s office.
	Draft and respond to official correspondence as directed.
	Coordinate communication between the Principal and staff, clients, or partners.
	Represent the Principal professionally in interactions with stakeholders when required.


Operational Effectiveness:


	Anticipate the needs of the Principal and take initiative to resolve issues.
	Ensure smooth daily operations by identifying gaps and proposing practical solutions.
	Maintain high standards of organisation, professionalism, and efficiency.
	Provide regular updates on tasks, schedules, and pending activities.


Performance Expectations


	Efficient management of the Principal&rsquo;s schedule and priorities.
	Timely completion of all assigned tasks and responsibilities.
	Accurate documentation and follow-up on action items.
	Professional representation of the Principal at all times.
	Demonstrated reliability, initiative, and commitment to excellence.


Qualifications and Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Communication, Social Sciences, or a related field.
	2 - 4 years of experience as a Personal Assistant, Executive Assistant, or similar role.
	Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and digital communication tools.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and work under pressure.


Key Skills and Attributes:


	Highly organised and detail-oriented.
	Proactive, resourceful, and solutions-driven.
	Strong time management and multitasking abilities.
	Professional, discreet, and trustworthy.
	Excellent interpersonal and communication skills.
	Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
	Strong sense of responsibility and accountability.


Working Conditions:


	Full-time role based in Abuja.
	May require occasional travel and extended working hours.
	A fast-paced work environment that requires flexibility and adaptability.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resumes to:&nbsp;amy@rensan.co.uk&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-05",
  "validThrough": "2026-03-20",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Ren San & Co"
    
  },

Personal Assistant (PA) Job at Ren San & Co.




        
    

        
        Ren San & Co. is a business support and full-service brand strategy firm. Our purpose is to help clients achieve their goals by developing and growing their brand, project, or event. We think of ourselves as more than just service providers, we are an extension of their team.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant (PA)

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Personal Assistant (PA) will provide comprehensive administrative, organisational, and personal support to the Principal.
	This role combines professional office assistance with personal and lifestyle management to ensure the smooth running of both work-related and personal activities.
	The PA will be responsible for managing schedules, coordinating communications, handling personal errands, and ensuring that daily priorities are effectively organised and executed.
	This position requires a highly proactive, dependable, and organised individual who can multitask, show initiative, and maintain professionalism and confidentiality at all times.


Key Responsibilities
Administrative and Executive Support:


	Manage the Principal’s daily calendar, appointments, meetings, and travel arrangements.
	Screen and manage emails, phone calls, and correspondence on behalf of the Principal.
	Prepare reports, presentations, letters, and other official documents as required.
	Organise meetings, take minutes, and ensure proper follow-up on action points.
	Maintain an efficient filing and documentation system (both digital and physical).
	Handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion.


Personal Assistance and Errands:


	Run personal errands such as shopping, banking, pickups, and deliveries.
	Purchase household and personal items as required.
	Coordinate home maintenance, vendors, and service providers.
	Handle bill payments, renewals, and other personal administrative tasks.
	Manage appointments such as medical visits, salon bookings, or family-related schedules.
	Provide general day-to-day personal support to reduce the Principal’s workload.


Communication and Stakeholder Engagement:


	Serve as the first point of contact for the Principal’s office.
	Draft and respond to official correspondence as directed.
	Coordinate communication between the Principal and staff, clients, or partners.
	Represent the Principal professionally in interactions with stakeholders when required.


Operational Effectiveness:


	Anticipate the needs of the Principal and take initiative to resolve issues.
	Ensure smooth daily operations by identifying gaps and proposing practical solutions.
	Maintain high standards of organisation, professionalism, and efficiency.
	Provide regular updates on tasks, schedules, and pending activities.


Performance Expectations


	Efficient management of the Principal’s schedule and priorities.
	Timely completion of all assigned tasks and responsibilities.
	Accurate documentation and follow-up on action items.
	Professional representation of the Principal at all times.
	Demonstrated reliability, initiative, and commitment to excellence.


Qualifications and Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Communication, Social Sciences, or a related field.
	2 - 4 years of experience as a Personal Assistant, Executive Assistant, or similar role.
	Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and digital communication tools.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and work under pressure.


Key Skills and Attributes:


	Highly organised and detail-oriented.
	Proactive, resourceful, and solutions-driven.
	Strong time management and multitasking abilities.
	Professional, discreet, and trustworthy.
	Excellent interpersonal and communication skills.
	Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
	Strong sense of responsibility and accountability.


Working Conditions:


	Full-time role based in Abuja.
	May require occasional travel and extended working hours.
	A fast-paced work environment that requires flexibility and adaptability.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resumes to: amy@rensan.co.uk using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Administrative Officer",
  "description": "Jami Engineering is a distinguished multidisciplinary engineering firm in Nigeria, offering robust civil, mechanical, and electrical engineering services to both public and private sector clients across diverse industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Administrative Officer is responsible for coordinating office activities, managing documentation, supporting engineering teams, and ensuring smooth day-to-day administrative operations within the firm.


Key Responsibilities
Office Administration:


	Manage daily office operations and ensure efficiency.
	Maintain office supplies and equipment.
	Handle incoming and outgoing correspondence (emails, letters, calls).
	Organize meetings, appointments, and company events.


Documentation &amp; Record Keeping:


	Maintain project files, engineering drawings, and contracts.
	Prepare reports, memos, and official documents.
	Ensure proper filing (physical and digital).
	Keep confidential company records secure.


Support to Engineering Team:


	Assist engineers with documentation and scheduling.
	Track project timelines and administrative requirements.
	Prepare tender documents and support bid submissions.
	Coordinate communication between engineers, clients, and contractors.


Procurement &amp; Vendor Coordination:


	Liaise with suppliers and vendors.
	Process purchase orders and invoices.
	Maintain records of materials and equipment deliveries.


Financial &amp; HR Support:


	Assist with payroll documentation.
	Maintain staff attendance records.
	Support recruitment processes (posting job ads, scheduling interviews).
	Assist in budget tracking and expense documentation.


Compliance &amp; Safety Documentation:


	Ensure company documents comply with regulatory requirements.
	Maintain safety records and certifications.
	Support audit preparations.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor&#39;s Degree with 4-7 years of experience.


Salary
N150,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their comprehensive Curriculum Vitae (CV) and a Cover Letter, addressed to &quot;The Human Resources Manager&quot; via:&nbsp;jamienergyltdacc@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
	Kindly note proximity to location is key!

Administrative Officer Job at Jami Engineering




        
    

        
        Jami Engineering is a distinguished multidisciplinary engineering firm in Nigeria, offering robust civil, mechanical, and electrical engineering services to both public and private sector clients across diverse industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Administrative Officer is responsible for coordinating office activities, managing documentation, supporting engineering teams, and ensuring smooth day-to-day administrative operations within the firm.


Key Responsibilities
Office Administration:


	Manage daily office operations and ensure efficiency.
	Maintain office supplies and equipment.
	Handle incoming and outgoing correspondence (emails, letters, calls).
	Organize meetings, appointments, and company events.


Documentation & Record Keeping:


	Maintain project files, engineering drawings, and contracts.
	Prepare reports, memos, and official documents.
	Ensure proper filing (physical and digital).
	Keep confidential company records secure.


Support to Engineering Team:


	Assist engineers with documentation and scheduling.
	Track project timelines and administrative requirements.
	Prepare tender documents and support bid submissions.
	Coordinate communication between engineers, clients, and contractors.


Procurement & Vendor Coordination:


	Liaise with suppliers and vendors.
	Process purchase orders and invoices.
	Maintain records of materials and equipment deliveries.


Financial & HR Support:


	Assist with payroll documentation.
	Maintain staff attendance records.
	Support recruitment processes (posting job ads, scheduling interviews).
	Assist in budget tracking and expense documentation.


Compliance & Safety Documentation:


	Ensure company documents comply with regulatory requirements.
	Maintain safety records and certifications.
	Support audit preparations.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree with 4-7 years of experience.


Salary
N150,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their comprehensive Curriculum Vitae (CV) and a Cover Letter, addressed to "The Human Resources Manager" via: jamienergyltdacc@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
	Kindly note proximity to location is key!
    

    

        Click here to Apply
